reasoning: The handler mtnc_plugin_dismiss_dialog is registered only under wp_ajax_mtnc_dismiss_dialog (line 34), not wp_ajax_nopriv_mtnc_dismiss_dialog, so unauthenticated access is blocked by WordPress core. The nonce 'mtnc_dismiss_nonce' is created via wp_create_nonce() and emitted only inside mtnc_admin_print_custom_styles(), which is hooked on admin_print_styles-{options_page} — a page protected by manage_options (add_menu_page at line 7 requires 'manage_options'). Low-privilege users (subscribers, customers) cannot load that page and therefore cannot obtain a valid nonce; the nonce check on line 20 exits immediately without one. The absence of an explicit current_user_can() call is a defense-in-depth gap but the attack is unreachable in practice. Additionally, the sink (update_option('maintenance_meta', ['mtnc_dismiss_dialog' => true])) only sets a boolean UI-dismissal flag — no attacker-controlled option name or arbitrary value is written, and maintenance mode state is controlled by a separate option.
